summary: Successfully tested the invoice edit immediate update bug fix. The fix (using spread operator in filterInvoices function) ensures invoice changes reflect immediately in the UI list without manual page refresh.
critical_code_review_comments: The fix correctly uses spread operator `[...invoices]` in filterInvoices function (line 1525) to create a new array reference, ensuring React state updates trigger proper re-renders
seed_data_creation: None - used existing invoice INV-000390 for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Invoice edit immediate update bug fix verified working. Test invoice INV-000390 was modified during testing (amount changed from 75000 to 99999). The filterInvoices function now properly creates a new array reference using spread operator.

test_details.bug_description: On the Sales 'View Invoices' page, when a user edits and saves an invoice, the changes were saved to the database but didn't reflect in the UI list without a manual page refresh
test_details.fix_applied: Modified filterInvoices function in /app/frontend/src/components/Sales.js to use spread operator `let filtered = [...invoices]` instead of `let filtered = invoices`
test_steps_performed: 1. Navigated to Sales -> View Invoices page
2. Noted original amount of first invoice (INV-000390): ₹75,000
3. Clicked Edit button on the invoice
6. Verified amount updated immediately to ₹99,999 in the list WITHOUT page refresh
7. Verified toast success message 'Invoice updated successfully!' appeared
8. Verified Total Revenue also updated correctly (increased by ₹24,999)
